name: Dau Khuon Xao Lan (Curried Tofu Saute)
recipeIngredient: 4 Tofu, firm, 3 oz cakes, cut crosswise into 1/2 inch slices
1 Green bell pepper, sliced into 1/4 inch thick pieces
1 Small onion, cut into thin wedges
1/2 tsp Dried chile flakes, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Vegetable stock and rice and set aside.
2. In a large skillet, heat 1-1/2 Tablespoons oil over medium-high heat until hot. Pat the tofu dry with paper towels and add enough of the tofu slices to fill the pan without overcrowding it. Cook until brown and crisp on both sides, about 3 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels. Repeat with the remaining tofu slices.
3. In a large skillet or wok over high heat, heat the remaining oil and cook the pepper and onion, stirring, for 1 minute. Add the curry paste and cook, stirring one minute. Add the stock, coconut milk, chili flakes, and tofu and simmer, stirring occasionally, to heat through and thicken slightly, about 3 minutes. Sprinkle with coriander and chopped peanuts and serve with rice.
